title: Turned a friend over to Instrumentality
display_title: Turned a friend over to Instrumentality
wikitext:
[]: Turned a friend over to Instrumentality.             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Feb 20, 2008 9:42 pm I recently convinced a friend to watch Evangelion after choking him with my fandom (blackmail) and he finished the series over the 3-day weekend. And he says he liked it and wants me to let him borrow my copy of EoE and a few Manga vol. Which I'll probably let him. This could mean that we might get a new member here soon. Is this not awesome or what?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Feb 20, 2008 9:47 pm YOU FOOL, OUR GOAL IS NOT TO FLOOD THE BOARD WITH NOOBS!

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Feb 20, 2008 9:51 pm Lol, when I look at your Avatar when I read that. He's pretty smart and good at analyzing things, he might be an good help.

[]:             Quote Post by macrossmaster » Wed Feb 20, 2008 10:22 pm My initial thought was "How did you tang them?" LOL

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Wed Feb 20, 2008 10:33 pm NAveryW wrote: YOU FOOL, OUR GOAL IS NOT TO FLOOD THE BOARD WITH NOOBS!

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Feb 20, 2008 10:40 pm BrikHaus wrote: NAveryW wrote: YOU FOOL, OUR GOAL IS NOT TO FLOOD THE BOARD WITH NOOBS!

[]:             Quote Post by Shiro » Wed Feb 20, 2008 10:55 pm Thank you, Sachi. Well, I suppose introducing as many people to Eva as possible is a good thing, let's just hope he goes and not at tanging and Freud and stuff.

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Thu Feb 21, 2008 12:03 am remember they have to be mature people. don't introduce people that will watch if after you say "there's sexual innuendo"

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Thu Feb 21, 2008 12:57 am I've turned like 2 friends to "Instrumentality" in the past two years. And now, they don't know about this site, but they're so hooked up to Evangelion you might as well call them "uber-nerds". And he better not be another birdy... But that's still awesome, considering the slow pace of members we get...

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Feb 21, 2008 1:05 am He's no Birdy (assuming there's something wrong with Birdy), he's even more mature about things then me. Though, he has had his moments. I doubt he'll join. If he does, he'll log in once in a blue moon.

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Thu Feb 21, 2008 1:07 am Do you know what username he'll be?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Feb 21, 2008 1:08 am He hasn't even shown any interest in joining. But if he's anything like me, he'll join in a matter of weeks. Especially after watching EoE. I didn't see EoE till AFTER I joined EMF.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Feb 21, 2008 2:59 am I've turned a lot of people on to NGE, but no real friends.

[]:             Quote Post by Themaninblack » Thu Feb 21, 2008 10:22 am Shiro wrote: Thank you, Sachi. Well, I suppose introducing as many people to Eva as possible is a good thing, let's just hope he goes and not at tanging and Freud and stuff.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Feb 21, 2008 11:32 am Themaninblack wrote: saying it faked its symbolism, untill EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Thu Feb 21, 2008 2:13 pm I have convinced two of my acquaintances to watch it also. They liked it, and they are talking about it like mad. One of them should be coming soon to this forum.

[]:             Quote Post by Themaninblack » Thu Feb 21, 2008 2:34 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Themaninblack wrote: saying it faked its symbolism, untill EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Fri Feb 22, 2008 1:37 am Themaninblack wrote: That it only pretended to be deep. AKA referrence a lot of obsecure shit, to make it look deep. He said he was wrong, but he's right when he says a lot of series do it.

[]:             Quote Post by mrpanda » Wed Feb 27, 2008 1:02 pm Im currently trying to get two friends to watch eva but i doubt they'll join the forums or even think about it as deeply as we do, they're just your generic anime watchers really.

[]:             Quote Post by Trigger's Elysium » Tue Mar 04, 2008 2:32 am mrpanda wrote: Im currently trying to get two friends to watch eva but i doubt they'll join the forums or even think about it as deeply as we do, they're just your generic anime watchers really.
